elements.name: vr
elements.description: Use <vr route.name /> to link to a route defined in src/routes.ts without repeating path strings. Route attributes are no-value shortcuts such as route.home and route.docs.

attributes.name: route.about
attributes.description: Basic app fixture About route reference.

elements.name: vr-alert
elements.description: Use <vr-alert> for important feedback messages. Variants such as variant.info and variant.danger are available as no-value Vanrot attributes.

elements.name: vr-avatar
elements.description: Use <vr-avatar> for a person, team, or account image/fallback. Variants such as variant.soft and variant.outline are available as no-value Vanrot attributes.

elements.name: vr-badge
elements.description: Use <vr-badge> for small status labels, counts, and metadata. Variants such as variant.success, variant.warning, and variant.outline are available as no-value Vanrot attributes.

elements.name: vr-breadcrumb
elements.description: Use <vr-breadcrumb> to show where the current page sits in the app. Put breadcrumb links or items inside it.

elements.name: vr-button
elements.description: Use <vr-button> for clickable actions. Variants such as variant.secondary, variant.ghost, and variant.danger are available as no-value Vanrot attributes.

elements.name: vr-card
elements.description: Use <vr-card> to group related content and actions in one surface. Variants such as variant.muted and variant.interactive are available as no-value Vanrot attributes.

elements.name: vr-checkbox
elements.description: Use <vr-checkbox> for an on/off form choice. Attributes such as checked, disabled, name, value, and aria-label are supported.

elements.name: vr-command-menu
elements.description: Use <vr-command-menu> for a searchable command palette or action picker. Compose it with input, list, group, item, shortcut, and empty-state child elements.

elements.name: vr-command-menu-empty
elements.description: Use <vr-command-menu-empty> for the message shown when no command matches the search.

elements.name: vr-command-menu-group
elements.description: Use <vr-command-menu-group> to group related command menu items under one section.

elements.name: vr-command-menu-input
elements.description: Use <vr-command-menu-input> as the search field for a command menu.

elements.name: vr-command-menu-item
elements.description: Use <vr-command-menu-item> for one selectable command or action.

elements.name: vr-command-menu-list
elements.description: Use <vr-command-menu-list> as the scrollable list that contains command groups and items.

elements.name: vr-command-menu-shortcut
elements.description: Use <vr-command-menu-shortcut> to show the keyboard shortcut for a command menu item.

elements.name: vr-container
elements.description: Use <vr-container> to constrain page content to a readable width.

elements.name: vr-dialog
elements.description: Use <vr-dialog> for modal content that needs focused attention. Attributes include open, size.sm, size.md, size.lg, aria-label, and aria-describedby.

elements.name: vr-dialog-close
elements.description: Use <vr-dialog-close> for the button or control that closes a dialog.

elements.name: vr-dialog-content
elements.description: Use <vr-dialog-content> for the main body of a dialog.

elements.name: vr-dialog-description
elements.description: Use <vr-dialog-description> for supporting text that explains the dialog.

elements.name: vr-dialog-footer
elements.description: Use <vr-dialog-footer> for dialog actions such as Cancel, Save, or Delete.

elements.name: vr-dialog-header
elements.description: Use <vr-dialog-header> to group the dialog title and description.

elements.name: vr-dialog-title
elements.description: Use <vr-dialog-title> for the main heading of a dialog.

elements.name: vr-dialog-trigger
elements.description: Use <vr-dialog-trigger> for the control that opens a dialog.

elements.name: vr-drawer
elements.description: Use <vr-drawer> for a side, top, or bottom panel that slides over the page. Attributes include open, side.left, side.right, side.top, side.bottom, and aria-label.

elements.name: vr-drawer-close
elements.description: Use <vr-drawer-close> for the button or control that closes a drawer.

elements.name: vr-drawer-content
elements.description: Use <vr-drawer-content> for the main body of a drawer.

elements.name: vr-drawer-description
elements.description: Use <vr-drawer-description> for supporting text that explains the drawer.

elements.name: vr-drawer-footer
elements.description: Use <vr-drawer-footer> for drawer actions or secondary controls.

elements.name: vr-drawer-header
elements.description: Use <vr-drawer-header> to group the drawer title and description.

elements.name: vr-drawer-title
elements.description: Use <vr-drawer-title> for the main heading of a drawer.

elements.name: vr-drawer-trigger
elements.description: Use <vr-drawer-trigger> for the control that opens a drawer.

elements.name: vr-dropdown
elements.description: Use <vr-dropdown> for a compact menu of actions or choices. Attributes include open, placement.*, and align.end.

elements.name: vr-dropdown-content
elements.description: Use <vr-dropdown-content> for the menu panel shown by a dropdown.

elements.name: vr-dropdown-item
elements.description: Use <vr-dropdown-item> for one selectable dropdown action.

elements.name: vr-dropdown-label
elements.description: Use <vr-dropdown-label> for a non-clickable section label inside dropdown content.

elements.name: vr-dropdown-separator
elements.description: Use <vr-dropdown-separator> to divide groups inside dropdown content.

elements.name: vr-dropdown-trigger
elements.description: Use <vr-dropdown-trigger> for the control that opens a dropdown.

elements.name: vr-empty-state
elements.description: Use <vr-empty-state> when a page, list, or search has no content to show.

elements.name: vr-footer
elements.description: Use <vr-footer> for the bottom area of a page or section.

elements.name: vr-form
elements.description: Use <vr-form> to group related form fields and submit actions. Attributes include disabled, pending, and name.

elements.name: vr-form-field
elements.description: Use <vr-form-field> to pair a label, help text, validation state, and a control. Attributes include required, name, label, and description.

elements.name: vr-grid
elements.description: Use <vr-grid> to lay out related cards, fields, or panels in columns.

elements.name: vr-header
elements.description: Use <vr-header> for the top area of a page or section. Put the logo, title, navigation, or actions inside it.

elements.name: vr-img
elements.description: Use <vr-img> for a styled image wrapper. Normal image attributes such as src and alt still apply.

elements.name: vr-input
elements.description: Use <vr-input> for text, email, password, or number fields. Attributes include type.*, size.*, tone.*, disabled, readonly, invalid, name, value, placeholder, and aria-label.

elements.name: vr-label
elements.description: Use <vr-label> for the visible label of a form control.

elements.name: vr-layout
elements.description: Use <vr-layout> as a page-level layout wrapper.

elements.name: vr-list
elements.description: Use <vr-list> for a styled list of related items. Put <vr-list-item> children inside it.

elements.name: vr-list-item
elements.description: Use <vr-list-item> for one item inside a Vanrot list.

elements.name: vr-loader
elements.description: Use <vr-loader> to show work in progress while content is loading.

elements.name: vr-nav
elements.description: Use <vr-nav> for primary or secondary navigation links.

elements.name: vr-outlet
elements.description: Use <vr-outlet> where child route content should render inside a layout template.

elements.name: vr-pagination
elements.description: Use <vr-pagination> to move through pages of data.

elements.name: vr-popover
elements.description: Use <vr-popover> for contextual content attached to a trigger. Attributes include open, side.*, and align.end.

elements.name: vr-popover-close
elements.description: Use <vr-popover-close> for the control that closes a popover.

elements.name: vr-popover-content
elements.description: Use <vr-popover-content> for the floating panel shown by a popover.

elements.name: vr-popover-description
elements.description: Use <vr-popover-description> for supporting text inside a popover.

elements.name: vr-popover-title
elements.description: Use <vr-popover-title> for the short heading inside a popover.

elements.name: vr-popover-trigger
elements.description: Use <vr-popover-trigger> for the control that opens a popover.

elements.name: vr-radio
elements.description: Use <vr-radio> for one choice in a radio group. Attributes include checked, size.md, disabled, name, and value.

elements.name: vr-radio-group
elements.description: Use <vr-radio-group> to group related radio choices. Attributes include disabled, name, value, and aria-label.

elements.name: vr-router
elements.description: Use <vr-router> once in the app layout to host route navigation and outlet behavior.

elements.name: vr-section
elements.description: Use <vr-section> to group a meaningful part of a page.

elements.name: vr-select
elements.description: Use <vr-select> for a dropdown form choice. Attributes include size.md, disabled, name, value, placeholder, and aria-label.

elements.name: vr-separator
elements.description: Use <vr-separator> to visually divide sections or menu groups.

elements.name: vr-sidebar
elements.description: Use <vr-sidebar> for persistent side navigation or supporting page content.

elements.name: vr-skeleton
elements.description: Use <vr-skeleton> as a placeholder while real content loads.

elements.name: vr-slider
elements.description: Use <vr-slider> for numeric values selected along a range. Attributes include size.md, disabled, min, max, step, and value.

elements.name: vr-src
elements.description: Use <vr-src> inside image or picture patterns when you need source-specific media.

elements.name: vr-stat
elements.description: Use <vr-stat> to show one metric, KPI, or compact value summary.

elements.name: vr-switch
elements.description: Use <vr-switch> for an on/off setting. Attributes include checked, size.md, disabled, name, value, and aria-label.

elements.name: vr-table
elements.description: Use <vr-table> for structured rows and columns. Compose it with table header, body, row, head, cell, caption, and footer child elements.

elements.name: vr-table-body
elements.description: Use <vr-table-body> for the main rows of a Vanrot table.

elements.name: vr-table-caption
elements.description: Use <vr-table-caption> to describe what a table contains.

elements.name: vr-table-cell
elements.description: Use <vr-table-cell> for one body cell in a Vanrot table row.

elements.name: vr-table-footer
elements.description: Use <vr-table-footer> for summary rows or table footer content.

elements.name: vr-table-head
elements.description: Use <vr-table-head> for one header cell in a Vanrot table row.

elements.name: vr-table-header
elements.description: Use <vr-table-header> for the header area of a Vanrot table.

elements.name: vr-table-row
elements.description: Use <vr-table-row> for one row in a Vanrot table.

elements.name: vr-tabs
elements.description: Use <vr-tabs> to switch between related panels of content. Compose it with tabs list, trigger, and panel child elements.

elements.name: vr-tabs-list
elements.description: Use <vr-tabs-list> to group the clickable tab triggers.

elements.name: vr-tabs-panel
elements.description: Use <vr-tabs-panel> for the content shown by one selected tab.

elements.name: vr-tabs-trigger
elements.description: Use <vr-tabs-trigger> for one clickable tab choice.

elements.name: vr-textarea
elements.description: Use <vr-textarea> for multi-line text input. Attributes include size.*, disabled, readonly, invalid, name, value, placeholder, and aria-label.

elements.name: vr-toast
elements.description: Use <vr-toast> to describe a transient notification. Compose it with item, title, description, close, and viewport child elements.

elements.name: vr-toast-close
elements.description: Use <vr-toast-close> for the control that dismisses a toast.

elements.name: vr-toast-description
elements.description: Use <vr-toast-description> for supporting text inside a toast.

elements.name: vr-toast-item
elements.description: Use <vr-toast-item> for one toast notification inside a viewport.

elements.name: vr-toast-title
elements.description: Use <vr-toast-title> for the short heading of a toast.

elements.name: vr-toast-viewport
elements.description: Use <vr-toast-viewport> as the region where toast items are rendered.

elements.name: vr-tooltip
elements.description: Use <vr-tooltip> for short helper text attached to a trigger.

elements.name: vr-tooltip-content
elements.description: Use <vr-tooltip-content> for the short helper text shown by a tooltip.

elements.name: vr-tooltip-trigger
elements.description: Use <vr-tooltip-trigger> for the element that shows a tooltip on hover or focus.

attributes.name: route.about
attributes.description: Basic app fixture About route reference.
